channel.title: by stephanie navarro
channel.description: Recent content on by stephanie navarro

item.title: I <3 NY
item.description:
A man wheeled a bright blue dresser across an intersection while a yellow taxi van squeezed around him. Restaurant workers dragged trash bags into alleyways. An Amazon employee dragged a wheelbarrow full of boxes behind him. A father checked his GPS again and again before waving his family in the right direction. Everyone looked tiny against the sprawl of the brick buildings and high rises all around them.

Welcome to New York City.

item.title: untitled 00
item.description:
my hand is pressed against your window.

let me in- I’m far from home and I’m so tired. I have questions I need to ask you:

was there love here? was there pain? how do I go back? how do I come home?

I watch your hand draw the blinds closed.

I used to ask God with all my heart to let me fly I used to ask God with all my heart to let me die.

item.title: stuck in my head
item.description:
there are times when my light feels dim when the words I say feel like they fall out of my mouth and thud onto the floor. there are times where i want to slip into the corners of my mind and stay there, spend my days staring at the wallks.

to know me is to know my pain- so I let those memories trickle down my throat let the pain branch through my body let that ancient storm swallow me,

item.title: The People Are Dying Here
item.description:
The people are dying there, the people are crying there.

Little boys run through the streets, crumpled bills in their pockets, selling fruit under the sky, minds worn from the hot sun.

Little girls push their legs closed, and keep their mouths shut, sneak their eyes open during prayer, fingertips burnt after flipping a tortilla.

They watch someone’s dreams play out on a neighbor’s TV. They’re hungry for more.

The children are dying there, the children are crying there.

item.title: finding you
item.description:
For so long I wove through the rooms of my life, Baby flowers in my hands. I planted them in every corner of the world Writing love letters on the petals Hoping one day someone would read them.

I started this poem before I knew you Before I knew what you looked like Or what your favorite colors were Before I knew if you loved the rain Or the sea.

item.title: behind the walls
item.description:
little woman, big girl.

the garden i grow is a home you’ll never see; a world you’ll never know.

I worked tirelessly, living only off of the hope that you would see it and smile.

I dream of it the day our hands touch again. I’ll show you all the fruit I grew out of my tears and you’ll pick me up and we’ll run across the world leaving my garden to rot.

item.title: you'll cry
item.description:
You’ll cry about it. You’ll cry until your eyes dry out, hot tears like the sidewalk in the summer on the day you crumpled to the ground, trying to remember where you were going and what you were saying and who you were loving and when did you lose them.

You’ll cry until your eyes are too swollen to stay open and your nose is as red as the sun pressing against your eyelids as you lay in the field by the lake where you leaned in to kiss the person who disappeared as quickly as they had arrived, leaving only the ghost of their fingers in your hair for memory.
